licensing.board: Alabama Licensing Board for General Contractors
requirements: Pass trade exam and business law exam
Financial statement required for projects over $150,000

lien.deadline: Within 6 months of last furnishing labor/materials

taxes.contractor_tax: Use tax may apply on materials

licensing.board: Alaska Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development
requirements: General Contractor Residential: projects up to $25,000

lien.deadline: Within 90 days of last furnishing labor/materials

taxes.sales_tax: No state sales tax (local taxes may apply)
taxes.contractor_tax: Contractor tax applies to certain projects

requirements: Pass trade exam and business management exam

lien.preliminary_notice: Required within 20 days of first furnishing

taxes.contractor_tax: Prime contractors exempt from sales tax on materials

requirements: Pass trade exam and law/business exam

lien.preliminary_notice: Required within 20 days of first furnishing (subcontractors/suppliers)

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor generally not taxable

licensing.board: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
requirements: Specialty trades require licenses (electrical, plumbing, HVAC)
Insurance recommended but not always required

lien.deadline: Within 3 months (residential) or 4 months (commercial) of last furnishing
lien.foreclosure: Within 1 year of filing lien (residential) or 2 years (commercial)

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor for new construction exempt

licensing.board: Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation
requirements: Certified license: work anywhere in Florida
Registered license: work in specific local jurisdiction
Pass trade exam and business finance exam

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor generally not taxable

licensing.board: NYC Department of Buildings (varies by locality)
requirements: NYC requires Home Improvement Contractor license
Home improvement projects over $200 require license in NYC
Insurance and bonding requirements vary by locality

lien.deadline: Within 8 months of last furnishing (residential: 4 months)

taxes.sales_tax: 4% state + local rates (up to 8.875% in NYC)
